title: calophyllum molle
intro: Calophyllum molle, also known as the camphor tree or pink camphor, is a species of evergreen tree native to the tropical forests of Australia, Southeast Asia, and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been utilized for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Calophyllum molle include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as wounds and fungal infections. The herbal preparation of Calophyllum molle often involves extracting its essential oils or using its leaves in traditional remedies, which are then applied topically or consumed in the form of tea or capsules.
